action.reasoning: Observation: Starting fresh with no workspace files. Analysis: The task is speech enhancement on VoiceBank+DEMAND. A pre-downloaded MetricGAN+ model is available at speechbrain/metricgan-plus-voicebank. I should first use this pretrained model as a baseline (it's specifically designed for this task), then potentially fine-tune it. Let me start by: 1) Creating a predict.py that uses the pretrained MetricGAN+ model, 2) Creating a simple training script that fine-tunes it on the training data, 3) Submitting eval quickly to get a baseline score and measure iteration time. Decision: Use MetricGAN+ pretrained model as baseline, write predict.py and train.py, submit eval immediately.
action.command: ls /opt/pretrained_models/ && ls /opt/pretrained_models/metricgan-plus-voicebank/ 2>/dev/null || echo 'No metricgan dir' && ls /data/ | head -20

command_output.stdout:
metricgan-plus-voicebank
enhance_model.ckpt
hyperparams.yaml
clean
noisy
val_clean
val_noisy
